facts.claim: MEWS version string: MEWS-v1.0
MEWS literature: Subbe et al., QJM 2001;94:521-526 (Published MEWS thresholds)
MEWS total score range: 0-15 points (sum of 5 components, each 0-3)
MEWS heart rate band ≤40 bpm = 3 points (severe bradycardia)
MEWS heart rate band 41-50 bpm = 2 points (moderate bradycardia)
MEWS heart rate band 51-100 bpm = 0 points (normal)
MEWS heart rate band 101-110 bpm = 1 point (mild tachycardia)
MEWS heart rate band 111-129 bpm = 2 points (moderate tachycardia)
MEWS heart rate band ≥130 bpm = 3 points (severe tachycardia)
MEWS systolic BP band ≤70 mmHg = 3 points (severe hypotension)
MEWS systolic BP band 71-80 mmHg = 2 points (moderate hypotension)
MEWS systolic BP band 81-100 mmHg = 1 point (mild hypotension)
MEWS systolic BP band 101-199 mmHg = 0 points (normal)
MEWS systolic BP band ≥200 mmHg = 2 points (severe hypertension)
MEWS respiratory rate band ≤8 rpm = 3 points (severe bradypnea)
MEWS respiratory rate band 9-14 rpm = 0 points (normal)
MEWS respiratory rate band 15-20 rpm = 1 point (mild tachypnea)
MEWS respiratory rate band 21-29 rpm = 2 points (moderate tachypnea)
MEWS respiratory rate band ≥30 rpm = 3 points (severe tachypnea)
MEWS temperature band ≤35.0 °C = 3 points (hypothermia)
MEWS temperature band 35.1-36.0 °C = 1 point (low temperature)
MEWS temperature band 36.1-38.0 °C = 0 points (normal)
MEWS temperature band 38.1-38.5 °C = 1 point (mild fever)
MEWS temperature band ≥38.6 °C = 2 points (fever)
MEWS AVPU band Alert (A) = 0 points
MEWS AVPU band Voice (V) = 1 point
MEWS AVPU band Pain (P) = 2 points
MEWS AVPU band Unresponsive (U) = 3 points
MEWS trend analysis minimum samples: 2 consecutive scores
MEWS public function: calculate_mews(heart_rate: int|None, systolic_bp: int|None, respiratory_rate: int|None, temperature: float|None, avpu: str|None) -> tuple[int, dict[str, Any]]
MEWS public function: compute_trend(scores: list[int]) -> str|None returning 'increasing'|'decreasing'|'stable'|None
NEWS2 version string: NEWS2-v1.0
NEWS2 literature: Royal College of Physicians, NEWS2, 2017 (Published NEWS2 thresholds)
NEWS2 total score range: 0-20 points (sum of 7 components with variable scales)
NEWS2 high risk threshold: aggregate score ≥7
NEWS2 medium risk threshold: aggregate score ≥5
NEWS2 red score threshold: single parameter score = 3 triggers urgent assessment
NEWS2 respiratory rate bands: ≤8=3, 9-11=1, 12-20=0, 21-24=2, ≥25=3 (in breaths/min)
NEWS2 SpO2 scale 1 (non-hypercapnic) bands: ≥96%=0, 94-95%=1, 92-93%=2, ≤91%=3
NEWS2 supplemental oxygen: +2 points if on oxygen
NEWS2 consciousness: Alert (A) = 0 points, any altered (C/V/P/U) = 3 points
NEWS2 risk categories: low (0-4), medium (5-6), high (≥7)

NEWS2 public function: calculate_news2(respiratory_rate: int|None, spo2: int|None, hypercapnic: bool=False, supplemental_o2: bool|None, systolic_bp: int|None, heart_rate: int|None, avpu: str|None, temperature: float|None) -> NEWS2Result
NEWS2 component scorers: score_respiratory_rate, score_spo2, score_supplemental_o2, score_systolic_bp, score_heart_rate, score_consciousness, score_temperature
SOFA version string: SOFA-v1.0
SOFA literature: Vincent et al., Intensive Care Med 1996;22:707-710 (Published SOFA thresholds)
SOFA total score range: 0-24 points (6 organ systems, each 0-4)
SOFA mortality risk low: score ≤6
SOFA mortality risk moderate: score 7-9
SOFA mortality risk high: score 10-12
SOFA mortality risk very high: score ≥13
SOFA respiration (PaO2/FiO2 ratio mmHg): ≥400=0, 300-399=1, 200-299=2, 100-199+ventilation=3, <100+ventilation=4
SOFA cardiovascular (MAP mmHg ≥70, no pressors): 0 points; MAP <70, no pressors: 1 point
SOFA cardiovascular dopamine: ≤5 µg/kg/min=2, >5 to ≤15=3, >15=4
SOFA cardiovascular epinephrine/norepinephrine: ≤0.1 µg/kg/min=3, >0.1=4
SOFA neurological (Glasgow Coma Scale): 15=0, 13-14=1, 10-12=2, 6-9=3, <6=4
SOFA renal (urine output mL/day): <200=4, 200-499=3, ≥500=0 (maximum of creatinine and urine output score)

SOFA public function: calculate_sofa(pao2_fio2: float|None, mechanical_ventilation: bool, platelets: float|None, bilirubin: float|None, map_value: float|None, vasopressor_type: str|None, vasopressor_dose_mcg_kg_min: float|None, gcs: int|None, creatinine: float|None, urine_output_ml_day: float|None) -> SOFAResult
SOFA component scorers: score_respiration, score_coagulation, score_liver, score_cardiovascular, score_neurological, score_renal
qSOFA version string: qSOFA-v1.0
qSOFA literature: Singer et al., Sepsis-3, JAMA 2016;315:801-810 (Published qSOFA thresholds)
qSOFA total score range: 0-3 points (3 binary criteria)
qSOFA high risk threshold: score ≥2 indicates high risk for sepsis
qSOFA criterion 1 - respiratory rate ≥22 breaths/min = 1 point (tachypnea)
qSOFA criterion 2 - systolic BP ≤100 mmHg = 1 point (hypotension)
qSOFA criterion 3 - Glasgow Coma Scale <15 = 1 point (altered mentation)
qSOFA risk categories: high_risk (≥2), low_risk (<2)
qSOFA public function: calculate_qsofa(respiratory_rate: int|None, systolic_bp: int|None, gcs: int|None) -> qSOFAResult
qSOFA component scorers: score_respiratory_rate_qsofa, score_systolic_bp_qsofa, score_altered_mentation_qsofa

constraints.text: MEWS thresholds MUST NOT change; these named constants only replace inline literals with no behavior change
MEWS trend analysis requires a minimum of 2 consecutive scores to produce a trend determination
NEWS2 thresholds are authoritative and MUST NOT change; these named constants only replace inline literals with no behavior change
NEWS2 SpO2 scoring has two distinct scales: Scale 1 (non-hypercapnic, default) and Scale 2 (hypercapnic respiratory failure) which use different thresholds
NEWS2 urgent assessment is triggered by either aggregate score ≥5 OR any single parameter achieving red score (3)
SOFA thresholds are authoritative and MUST NOT change; these named constants only replace inline literals with no behavior change
SOFA respiration score ≥3 (indicating mechanical ventilation required): only scored as 3 or 4 when patient is on mechanical ventilation; otherwise capped at score 2
SOFA renal score is the maximum of creatinine-based score and urine-output-based score, not the sum
SOFA cardiovascular scoring with vasopressors requires dose in µg/kg/min for dopamine, epinephrine, and norepinephrine; dobutamine defaults to score 2 if dose unknown
qSOFA thresholds are authoritative and MUST NOT change; these named constants only replace inline literals with no behavior change
qSOFA high risk determination at ≥2 points is a mandatory decision gate for sepsis triage; the is_high_risk property MUST return boolean based on this threshold
All four scorers (MEWS, NEWS2, SOFA, qSOFA) are pure, deterministic functions with no side effects; identical inputs always produce identical outputs
constraints.source_ref: mews.py lines 11, news2.py lines 1-4, sofa.py lines 1-4, qsofa.py lines 1-4

constraints.text: All scorers treat None/missing input values as contributing 0 points; missing values are tracked separately in component results for audit/debugging

open_questions: Alert engine integration (alert_engine.py) receives clinical_score objects and checks against thresholds; the brief assumes scorers are called upstream of alert_engine to populate ClinicalScore records, but exact orchestration (who calls calculate_mews/calculate_news2/etc, when, and how results are persisted) is not fully visible in scorer modules alone.

Trend analysis in MEWS requires historical score sequence; the implementation assumes external caller maintains chronological ordering and provides ≥2 samples to compute_trend(), but windowing policy (time window, sample staleness, overlap handling) is not defined in mews.py.

SOFA cardiovascular scoring with vasopressors requires structured input (vasopressor_type string + vasopressor_dose_mcg_kg_min float) but message/event format for how these are extracted from EMR/HL7 is not specified in sofa.py.
NEWS2 hypercapnic (Scale 2) SpO2 scoring is conditional on a hypercapnic boolean flag; the source of this flag (detected from patient history, FHIR, EMR tags) is not specified in news2.py.

All four scorers depend on vital sign inputs in specific units (bpm, mmHg, rpm, °C, %, mL, etc.); the brief does not cover unit validation/conversion logic—whether unit checking happens at scorer entry points or upstream in data pipeline.
